title: theanales
intro: The order theanales is a taxonomic rank that consists of flowering plants that have evolved to live in shaded environments with low light levels. This group includes plants that have adapted to survive in these conditions through unique morphological and physiological features. Examples of families within the theanales order include Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and poinsettia, and Cyperaceae, which comprises sedges. Theanales is one of the many orders in the larger plant kingdom, providing a more detailed classification of plant diversity.

families.family_desc: Philesiaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Asterales, commonly known as the Fritillaries or Coralroots.
Emblingiaceae is a small family of flowering plants, commonly known as the emblings, native to the subtropical and tropical regions of the Pacific and Indian Oceans.
